metadata.description: IPv4: 32-bit address in dotted decimal notation. Class A/B/C/D/E addresses. NAT to alleviate address exhaustion. Python code example for IP address representation (using a class to represent an IP address): ```python class IPAddress: def __init__(self, ip_str): self.ip = [int(part) for part in ip_str.split('.')] def __str__(self): return '.'.join(map(str, self.ip)) # Example usage: ip = IPAddress('192.0.2.1') print(ip) # Output: 192.0.2.1 ``` This code defines a simple class to represent an IP address in dotted decimal notation and includes a brief example of how it can be used.
